steps.title: Identify the unfair dismissal
steps.content: The passage argues that disco is far less superficial than many people believe. It uses Gloria Gaynor to show that disco can include concerns about community and identity, not only love and heartbreak. It also stresses that love and heartbreak are hardly unique to disco, so ridiculing disco for that subject matter is unfair.

steps.title: Reject unsupported histories
steps.content: Choice A says scholars ignored disco, but the passage cites scholars defending it. Choice B invents a shift from superficial songs to serious songs over time. Choice D reverses the history by saying disco created a Black women's musical tradition. Choice C fits the claim that disco was dismissed for subject matter found in other genres too. The answer is C.
